key_insight: A PFEM mesh-adaptation strategy preserves sharp multiphase interfaces by forcing interface segments into the Delaunay triangulation, decoupling topology changes from mesh resolution.
engineering_relevance: This enables robust, fully Lagrangian simulation of many immiscible phases with sharper geometry and less mesh-driven artificial diffusion, which is valuable for multiphase CFD and interface dynamics.
method: Assign a node-empty disk to each interface segment so it becomes part of the Delaunay mesh.
Adapt the mesh dynamically while keeping the discretized interface sharp without classical constrained triangulation.
Allow sub-grid physical models to control topological changes instead of the user-defined element size.
evidence_signal: The framework is validated on standard multiphase benchmarks and a challenging 16-phase Rayleigh-Taylor case, matching references while using a very low node count.
watch_item: The approach likely requires careful implementation of the interface-based mesh update and sub-grid topology model to remain robust as phase count and interface complexity increase.
paper_title: Topology-Preserving Mesh Adaptation for Sharp-Interface Multiphase PFEM
